---
title: "Cairngorm Snow"
description: "A snow persistence dataset for the Cairngorms National Park, Scotland"
format: html
jpuyter: python3
---

## About This Data

This data was generated using the [SPUN](https://github.com/leamhowe/SPUN) deep learning model for mapping snow cover from [Sentinel-2](https://sentinel.esa.int/web/sentinel/missions/sentinel-2) imagery. The model was used to create Snow Cover Fraction (SCF) maps for the entire Sentinel-2 archive, all of these were combined and normalised by cloud-free view to give an estimate of snow persistence.

<!-- ### Access Links

You can access the raw hosted files here:

*   **Raw COG:** [`SP_CNP_AllYears_pooled_2016-2026.tif`](data/SP_CNP_AllYears_pooled_2016-2026_COG.tif)
*   *STAC JSON: (Coming soon)* -->

---
<!-- 
## Web Visualization

*Once you generate your STAC JSON (as discussed previously) and place it in the `data/` folder, you can embed the Development Seed STAC Map directly into this Quarto page using an iframe like the one below:*

```html
<!-- Uncomment and update the URL once your JSON is hosted -->

<!-- <iframe 
  src="https://developmentseed.org/stac-map/?url=https://leamhowe.github.io/data/stac_item.json" 
  width="100%" 
  height="600px" 
  style="border: none; border-radius: 8px;">
</iframe> --> -->

```{python}
#| label: interactive-cog-map
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

import leafmap.leafmap as leafmap

# 1. Initialize the map
m = leafmap.Map()

# 2. Add your preferred basemap (OpenTopoMap)
m.add_basemap("OpenTopoMap")

# 3. Stream your hosted COG
cog_url = "https://github.com/leamhowe/leamhowe.github.io/blob/main/data/SP_CNP_AllYears_pooled_2016-2026_COG.tif"
m.add_cog_layer(
    url=cog_url, 
    name="Pooled Data (2016-2026)",
    colormap_name="mako" # Optional: apply a colormap if it's single-band data
)

# 4. Render the widget directly into the Quarto HTML output
m
```
